title: DSDS Token and Token Group Definitions
description: Docs for individual design tokens and nested token groups. Token values, aliases, and type info live in the W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) file. DSDS documents only the purpose, guidelines, and organization. Token groups are flexible units: a full token collection, a token family (e.g., all color tokens), or a sub-family (e.g., a single hue family). Groups can hold tokens, nested groups, or both. This forms a hierarchy that mirrors how design systems organize tokens. Tokens and token groups omit the display `name` property. The `identifier` itself serves as the display label. Description and status are optional to keep things terse at scale. The `metadata` object holds optional fields: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

token.description: Docs for a single design token: identifier, type, use cases, guidelines, and accessibility notes. The display `name` property is absent. The `identifier` itself serves as the display label. Description and status are optional. The `metadata` object holds optional fields: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. When a token sits inside a token group and omits its own status field, the parent group's status applies.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a token.

identifier.description: The token identifier. It follows no fixed pattern, so it can support DTCG and design tool naming styles that use dots, slashes, or other separators (e.g., 'color-text-primary', 'color.text.primary', 'color/text/primary'). It also serves as the human-readable display label.

name.description: Optional display name (e.g., 'Primary Text'). When omitted, the `identifier` serves as the display label — the uniform rule for all entities is: display `name` when present, otherwise `identifier`.

tokenType.description: The type of token aligned with the DTCG spec (e.g., 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'cubicBezier', 'shadow').

description.description: What this token represents and when to use it. Optional, so keep it terse at scale. CommonMark supported.

source.description: Points to the W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not copy token values. The DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type info.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this token's definition.

path.description: The dot-path to this token within the DTCG file (e.g., 'color.text.primary').

metadata.description: Optional metadata fields, keyed by name: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this token, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Tokens accept the general kinds only (guidelines, useCases, accessibility, content, sections). The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters, so tools SHOULD keep it for display.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured docs for agents (AI/LLM) only. This is a separate space that accepts the same document block kinds as `documentBlocks`. Use it for guidance that would be noise for human readers: generation limits, ways to tell confusable entities apart, and machine-checkable rules with evidence. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing docs. Agents SHOULD read both arrays — human-facing blocks first, then these.

tokenGroup.description: A group of related design tokens. Token groups are flexible units: a full collection, a family (e.g., all color tokens), a sub-family (e.g., a single hue with its grade scale), or any other logical grouping. Groups can nest to form a deep hierarchy. A top-level group might stand for 'color' and hold child groups for 'color-text', 'color-background', and 'color-border', each with its own tokens. This nesting mirrors how real design systems organize tokens into families and sub-families. The display `name` property is absent. The `identifier` itself serves as the display label. Description and status are optional. The `metadata` object holds optional fields: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, and links. When a child omits its own status field, the parent group's status applies.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a token-group.

identifier.description: The token group identifier. It follows no fixed pattern, so it can support DTCG and design tool naming styles (e.g., 'color-text', 'color.text', 'color/text'). It also serves as the human-readable display label.

name.description: Optional display name (e.g., 'Primary Text'). When omitted, the `identifier` serves as the display label — the uniform rule for all entities is: display `name` when present, otherwise `identifier`.

description.description: What this token group covers and how it is organized. Optional, so keep it terse at scale. CommonMark supported.

tokenType.description: When all tokens in this group share the same DTCG token type, declare it here so you don't repeat it on each child. Each token in children MAY override this value. Common values: 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'number', 'shadow'.

source.description: Points to the W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not copy token values. The DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type info.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this group's definition.

path.description: The dot-path to this group within the DTCG file (e.g., 'color.text').

children.description: An ordered array of tokens and/or nested token groups inside this group. Each item MUST be either a token or a tokenGroup. Order often shows a meaningful progression (e.g., lightest to darkest, or smallest to largest), so it matters and tools SHOULD keep it for display. Children inherit tokenType and status from this group when they omit their own values.

metadata.description: Optional metadata fields, keyed by name: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this token group, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. It accepts the general kinds only (guidelines, useCases, accessibility, content, sections). Use this array for docs that apply to the group as a whole. Put docs for individual tokens on the token inside `children`.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured docs for agents (AI/LLM) only. This is a separate space that accepts the same document block kinds as `documentBlocks`. Use it for guidance that would be noise for human readers: generation limits, ways to tell confusable entities apart, and machine-checkable rules with evidence. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing docs. Agents SHOULD read both arrays — human-facing blocks first, then these.
